MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO TAX COSTS (TRANSACTION ID # 62648127) FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SAN FRANCISCO ETHICS COMMISSION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED AS TO ALL COSTS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN AND PLAINTIFF LOU ANN BASSAN'S REQUEST IN HER OPPOSITION PAPER FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. $1,110.55 OF MS. BASSAN'S CLAIMED COSTS ARE TAXED. WHILE MS. BASSAN'S ERROR IN INITIALLY FILING HER WRIT IN THE COURT OF APPEAL IS UNDERSTANDABLE, HER INCURRENCE OF THE COURT OF APPEAL FILING FEE WAS NOT REASONABLY NECESSARY PER THE COSTS STATUTES CASE LAW. MS. BASSAN'S COSTS FOR EXHIBITS AND COURT REPORTER TRANSCRIPTS ARE NOT ALLOWABLE PER THE COSTS STATUTES. TREATING MS. BASSAN'S REQUEST FOR FEES AS A PROPERLY FILED CROSS-MOTION, THE REQUEST IS DENIED BECAUSE MS. BASSAN HAS MADE NO SHOWING THAT HER LITIGATION SUCCESS CONFERRED A BENEFIT ON ANYONE OTHER THAN HERSELF. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And (Real Party In Interest Leeann Phelham, In Her Official Capacity As Executive Director Of San Francisco Ethics Commission)
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lou Ann Bassan, 415-753-8315 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Joshua D. White, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4700 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CCSF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ETHICS COMMISSION's Motion To Tax Costs And (Real Party In Interest Leeann Phelham, In Her Official Capacity As Executive Director Of San Francisco Ethics Commission)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to tax costs is granted as to all costs sought to be stricken and plaintiff Lou Ann Bassan's request in her opposition paper for an award of attorney's fees is denied. $1,110.55 of Ms. Bassan's claimed costs are taxed. While Ms. Bassan's error in initially filing her writ in the Court of Appeal is understandable, her incurrence of the Court of Appeal filing fee was not reasonably necessary per the costs statutes case law. Ms. Bassan's costs for exhibits and court reporter transcripts are not allowable per the costs statutes. Treating Ms. Bassan's request for fees as a properly filed cross-motion, the request is denied because Ms. Bassan has made no showing that her litigation success conferred a benefit on anyone other than herself.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And (Real Party In Interest Leeann Phelham, In Her Official Capacity As Executive Director Of San Francisco Ethics Commission)
Off Calendar Dec-13-2018 Continued To Jan-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua White, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Telephone 415-554-4661, appearing for Defendant,
CCSF;
No other appearance.

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Respondents' motion to tax costs is continued to January 10, 2019 to give respondents an opportunity to file a reply brief as a result of the late-filed opposition by petitioner Lou Ann Bassan. If respondents choose to file a reply brief, they must do so no later than January 8, 2019. Defendant's counsel to deliver to plaintiff a copy of the reply brief by January 8, 2019.

Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/HEK)

Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lou Ann Bassan, Plaintiff, 415.753.8315, appearing in pro per;
Joshua S. White, Esq. of Office of the SF City Attorney, 415.554.4661, appearing for the Defendant.

Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan's Notice of Motion and Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate.

Matter called. Ms. Bassan presented an update for the Court with regard to the documentation/information recently provided to her. At the conclusion of the update by Ms. Bassan and discussions with regard to setting another hearing date, the Court confirmed the order previously granting the writ of mandate and declined to set a future date. The order granting the writ is signed in open court.

Ms. Bassan addressed the elections being less than three weeks away. The Court informed Ms. Bassan that she could bring an ex parte application. The Court requested that counsel work together on a form of order noting that the writ of mandate required Ms. Pelham to perform the review as if the papers were timely filed. The Court further stated that if counsel could not agree on the language in the order that they may submit separate orders.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lou Ann Bassan, Tel. No. 1-415-753-8315, Petitioner appearing in pro-per;
Atty. Joshua S. White, Tel. No. 1-415-554-4661 appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D; Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan's petition for writ of mandate is continued to October 15, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 for the ethic's commission headed by Ms. Pelham to review and determine if there are additional documentation that is needed for certain contributors that may have have insufficient information. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk
